items.title: Khaled: Defence self-reliance key to national security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Countries should not depend on others for their defence and must instead build their own military capabilities, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. Khaled was responding to a call by United States Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth for Asian allies and partners to increase defence spending to 3.5 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP), saying the policy is a fair request and aligned with Malaysia's longstanding approach to defence self-reliance. It is a good policy and a clear policy of the US," he said.

items.title: Four more men trapped in semi-submerged Laos cave freed: rescuers
items.summary: VIENTIANE: Four more men trapped in a semi-submerged Laos cave for 10 days have been freed, rescuers said, after one was successfully brought to the surface a day earlier, but two remained missing. "4 victim is out," Lee Kian Lie, a Malaysian rescue diver, told AFP via Messenger. The Thailand Rescue Diver Facebook page said in a post that "rescue officials were able to bring out four more people trapped" at about 3:10 pm (0810 GMT) on Saturday.

items.title: Engineer found dead in locked car died of carbon monoxide poisoning
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A 37-year-old engineer who was found dead inside a parked car along Jalan Sungai Dua yesterday died of carbon monoxide poisoning. Timur Laut acting district police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said the cause of death was determined following a post-mortem at the Penang Hospital forensic department today. "The post-mortem found that the victim died due to carbon monoxide poisoning, and there were no elements of foul play.

items.title: Anwar: Ministry savings redirected to programmes that benefit the people
items.summary: PENAMPANG: The Madani Government has imposed strict cost-cutting measures across ministries, with all savings channelled into programmes that directly benefit the people. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said cuts would focus on non-essential spending, while key sectors such as education, healthcare and basic infrastructure, including in Sabah, would remain unaffected. Anwar said the cost-cutting measures include limiting ministers' overseas trips, except for mandatory meetings under international platforms such as Asean, Brics and Apec.

items.title: Former MACC chief Azam Baki to file defamation suit against businessman Albert Tei next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said he will file a defamation suit against businessman Albert Tei next week, following allegations that he threatened and attempted to prevent the disclosure of claims linked to the Sabah corruption scandal. Azam told Astro Awani today that the legal action was intended to defend his reputation and that of the anti-graft agency, while ensuring the public was made aware of the facts surrounding the matter. “The public needs to know the facts.

items.title: PKR election director reshuffle a strategic move, says AMK
items.summary: PADANG RENGAS: The change in PKR's election director post is part of a strategic restructuring exercise aimed at strengthening the party machinery ahead of the next election. On the appointment of PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari as the party's election director alongside Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Muhammad Kamil said both leaders possess extensive experience in managing election operations and organisation. "Previously, Amirudin served as election operations director, giving him the knowledge and experience required.

items.title: Don't politicise federal funds, Umno Youth tells PH [WATCH]
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Umno Youth has hit back at Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders over claims that Johor's recent development was largely driven by federal funding. "The RM14.6 billion allocation repeatedly highlighted by PH leaders comprised a mix of announced projects, operational expenditure and federal administrative spending, and should not be portrayed as direct financial assistance to the Johor state government," he said in a Facebook post. "These are federal government expenses in Johor.

items.title: Mustapha: RM1.5bil interim payment proves federal govt's commitment to Sabah
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The increase in Sabah's interim special grant payment from RM600 million to RM1.5 billion reflects the federal government's continued commitment to addressing the state's rights claims in a gradual, structured and responsible manner. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud said the increase underscored the Madani government's commitment under Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to ensure Sabah's rights continue to receive due attention. Mustapha said that before the change of federal government in 2022, Sabah had received only about RM26.7 million annually for decades, despite its 40 per cent revenue entitlement being enshrined in the Federal Constitution.

items.title: ‘A true patriot’: Dzulkefly hails IJN cardiothoracic surgeon Dr Jeswant Dillon’s historic AATS induction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Prof Datuk Seri Dr Jeswant Dillon’s induction as the first Malaysian into the American Association for Thoracic Surgery (AATS) represents the highest form of recognition for his excellence, dedication and contributions to the field of cardiothoracic surgery. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said this achievement was not attained overnight but is the result of the surgeon’s ongoing commitment and years of devoted service in advancing medical care. “On behalf of the Ministry of Health and the government, I extend my deepest respect to Dr Jeswant for this truly well-deserved recognition.

items.title: PKR election director reshuffle is about strategy, not personalities, says Youth chief
items.summary: PADANG RENGAS, May 30 — The change in PKR’s election director post is part of a strategic restructuring exercise aimed at strengthening the party machinery ahead of the next election. On the appointment of PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari as the party’s election director alongside Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Muhammad Kamil said both leaders possess extensive experience in managing election operations and organisation. “Previously, Amirudin served as election operations director, giving him the knowledge and experience required.

items.title: Minister says RM1.5b Sabah interim grant shows federal government is listening
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 30 — The increase in the special interim grant for Sabah, from RM600 million to RM1.5 billion, demonstrates the federal government’s ongoing commitment to addressing the state’s rightful claims gradually and responsibly. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak), Datuk Mustapha Sakmud, said the announcement by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim marks a significant achievement, reflecting the Madani Government’s determination to ensure that Sabah’s interests continue to receive due attention. Mustapha said the issue of Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement has dragged on for decades.

items.title: Search teams retrieve camera memory chips to track missing hiker
items.summary: Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the task was being jointly undertaken by a Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) protection team and 16 rescue personnel. "The protection team, together with the rescue team, will retrieve surveillance camera chips around the trail area to monitor whether the victim can be detected through the surveillance camera route. Meanwhile, the Perak Mountain Guides Association, in a Facebook post, said the operation to locate Jaslinda Saludin, 49, had also received support from out-of-state mountain guides who joined the search mission.

items.title: Anwar says government cuts its own spending to channel more aid to rakyat
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the Madani Government has implemented strict cost-cutting measures across ministries, with the savings fully redirected towards programmes and initiatives that benefit the people. He said allocations for ministries would be reduced for non-essential spending, although critical sectors such as education, healthcare and basic infrastructure, including in Sabah, would be spared. Anwar said the cost-cutting measures include limiting ministers’ overseas trips, except for mandatory meetings under international platforms such as Asean, Brics and Apec.

items.title: Ewon hails RM1.5bil interim payment for Sabah
items.summary: PENAMPANG: United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (Upko) president Datuk Ewon Benedick has welcomed Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's commitment to implement Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement as provided under the Federal Constitution. The commitment includes increasing the interim payment to Sabah from RM600 million to RM1.5 billion. Ewon, who is also Sabah Deputy Chief Minister III and Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister, said the announcement reflected the federal government's commitment to progressively fulfil Sabah's claims relating to its revenue rights.

items.title: Sabah's growth exceeds projections, KKIA upgrade to be expedited – Anwar
items.summary: PENAMPANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said rapid growth in Sabah's development and tourism sector has surpassed earlier projections, prompting the federal government to expedite infrastructure upgrading works, particularly at Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA). "However, Sabah's growth has surpassed normal projections and recorded development that is truly commendable," he said when launching the state-level Kaamatan Festival 2026 at Hongkod Koisaan, Kadazandusun Cultural Association (KDCA) here today. Also present were Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Seri Dr Joachim Gunsalam, and KDCA president Tan Sri Joseph Pairin Kitingan.

items.title: KL book fair reflects reader demand, not exclusion, says organiser
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Organisers of the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair (KLIBF) 2026 have defended the event against criticism over the diversity of books, publishers, and authors featured at the annual fair, saying participation remains open to all and is ultimately driven by market demand. One user, naimtamdjis, questioned the event's international credentials, arguing that major international publishers and globally recognised authors were largely absent while Malay-language titles appeared to dominate the fair. Others, however, defended the fair.

items.title: Raja of Perlis launches Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Free Trade Zone-Perlis Inland Port
items.summary: PADANG BESAR, May 30 — The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, today graced and officiated the launch of the Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Free Trade Zone-Perlis Inland Port (PIP) here. In his royal address, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said the development of PIP as a free trade zone was not merely a symbol of physical development but a strategic step towards strengthening Perlis’ and Malaysia’s position as a regional trade and logistics gateway, anchored on intra-Asia land transportation, particularly the Pan-Asia railway network. Also gracing the event was the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail.

items.title: Search for missing hiker intensified with 16 more rescuers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing female hiker Jaslinda Saludin, has been intensified with the deployment of 16 additional rescuers. The Perak Fire and Rescue Department said the additional rescuers comprise six members of the department's Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team (Storm), two Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) protection team members, five Forestry Department personnel, and three Forestry Mountain Guides (MGPs). It said all personnel were flown to the operation site aboard an AgustaWestland AW139 helicopter this morning.

items.title: Loke: Govt reviewing laws, surveillance to tackle railway cable theft
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the matter has been discussed with the Home Ministry to enforce stricter legal action against such offences, which are viewed as a national security issue. He said cable theft cannot be treated as an ordinary theft because it directly impacts the smooth operation of train services and public safety. Loke said among the main challenges in addressing the issue are high copper prices and the country's extensive railway network, which spans over 1,000km.

items.title: Loke: Railway cable theft is a national security issue, govt eyeing tougher laws, tech solutions
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke said his ministry is looking into the use of technology to detect and prevent cable theft activities from recurring, as these thefts are a major issue for the service, particularly in the northern region. Loke said his ministry is also discussing with the Home Ministry (KDN) about tightening the laws against cable theft because, despite actions being taken regarding these cases, they are still rampant. Loke said that his team is also examining the practices and appropriate solutions used by other countries to address the same issue, considering that cable theft cases also occur in countries with rail systems.

items.title: Suspected 'tekong darat', nine undocumented migrants held after chase ends in crash
items.summary: Southeast Brigade General Operations Force (GOF) commander, Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain, said the 21-year-old Malaysian is believed to be a tekong darat, a term used locally for individuals who transport or harbour undocumented migrants. GOF personnel then pursued the vehicle. "GOF personnel subsequently detained the driver and nine passengers," he said.

items.title: Cops: Man found dead in locked car outside Penang ice cream shop died of carbon monoxide poisoning
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 30 — A man found dead inside a car parked outside an ice cream shop along Jalan Sungai Dua here yesterday was confirmed to have died from carbon monoxide poisoning. Northeast District deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said the cause of death of the 37-year-old man was determined following a post-mortem examination conducted at the Forensic Department of Penang Hospital today. “The victim is also believed to have died about five days before his body was discovered yesterday.

items.title: New Perlis inland port to strengthen Malaysia's role as regional logistics hub
items.summary: PADANG BESAR: The government is confident that the development of the Perlis Inland Port (PIP), known as the Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Free Trade Zone, will contribute significantly to the Malaysia-Thailand bilateral trade target of US$30 billion (RM119 billion) by 2027. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the strategic development will strengthen Malaysia's position as a regional logistics hub, particularly in the northern border area. He said the PIP development would strengthen cross-border trade relations and expand the economic network into the Mekong region, China, and Central Asia.

items.title: Rescuers retrieve trail camera data in search for missing hiker Jaslinda on Gunung Batu Putih
items.summary: TAPAH, May 30 — A team has been deployed to retrieve surveillance camera chips around the Gunung Batu Putih hiking trail here to monitor whether a female hiker reported missing for the past week was detected passing through the area. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the task was jointly undertaken by a Protection Team from the National Parks and Wildlife Department (Perhilitan) and 16 rescue personnel. “The Protection Team, together with the rescue team, will retrieve surveillance camera chips around the trail area to monitor whether the victim can be detected through the surveillance camera route.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm and heavy rain warning for KL, five states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong winds warning for five states and Kuala Lumpur until 5pm today. The department in a statement said the affected areas in Pahang are Bentong and Bera, while in Selangor the warning covers Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat. In Sabah, the affected areas are in Ranau, Kunak, Lahad Datu, Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran, Sandakan, Kota Marudu and Pitas.

items.title: Sabah steps up efforts to position Kaamatan as an international tourism attraction, says DCM
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 30 — Sabah is intensifying efforts to promote the Kaamatan Festival as an international tourism product through strategic collaboration with various agencies and industry players to showcase the state’s unique cultural heritage on the global stage. Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Seri Joachim Gunsalam said the initiative is being implemented through cooperation between the State Government, Sabah Tourism Board, Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture and the Kadazandusun Cultural Association (KDCA). Dr Joachim, who is also the State-Level Kaamatan Festival organising chairman, said that through the strategic measures, Kaamatan is not only celebrated as the cultural heritage of Sabah’s people but is also promoted as a premier tourism product capable of attracting more domestic and international visitors.

items.title: Fadhlina encourages Malaysians to embrace reading culture
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek is encouraging Malaysians to invest in books and cultivate a reading habit, stressing the importance of reading in building a knowledgeable society. Speaking during an interview at the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair (KLIBF) 2026 today, Fadhlina said Malaysia must ensure equitable access to books if it aspires to become a World Book Capital. In conjunction with the event, Fadhlina also launched Kelab Perempuan Membaca (KPM).

items.title: Anwar says Sabah’s growth has surpassed projections, KKIA upgrade to be expedited
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said rapid growth in Sabah’s development and tourism sector has surpassed earlier projections, prompting the federal government to expedite infrastructure upgrading works, particularly at Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA). However, Sabah’s growth has surpassed normal projections and recorded development that is truly commendable,” he said when launching the state-level Kaamatan Festival 2026 at Hongkod Koisaan, Kadazandusun Cultural Association (KDCA) here today. Also present were Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Seri Dr Joachim Gunsalam, and KDCA president Tan Sri Joseph Pairin Kitingan.

items.title: End of the road for Aeroline in KL after bus operator loses Corus and TRX footholds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Aeroline is set to pull out of Kuala Lumpur entirely, ending more than two decades of operations in the city after losing access to two key locations that had served as gateways for its premium coach services. In a statement on Facebook today, the bus operator said Kuala Lumpur would no longer serve as a landing point for its coaches, adding that regulatory requirements now restrict operations to licensed terminals. However, Aeroline said it received a directive on May 13 requiring it to operate only from licensed terminals.

items.title: Perlis Inland Port set to boost Malaysia-Thailand trade and regional connectivity, says Loke
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the close ties between Malaysia and Thailand have long been the foundation of economic development in the border areas of both countries, and the PIP would further strengthen these economic relations through more efficient, faster and integrated cross-border trade facilitation. PIP has the potential to become a key land trade gateway linking ASEAN countries with broader markets in East Asia and Eurasia,” he said. He said this at the opening ceremony of the Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Free Commercial Zone at PIP today, officiated by the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail.

items.title: Unity minister calls for wiser digital culture in Wesak Day message
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang has called on Malaysians to uphold human values, strengthen unity and progress towards a more ethical, well-mannered and wise digital society. “In today’s digital world, unity is tested not only in the real world but also on social media. (While) a single post can unite society, a slander can destroy national harmony,” he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Malaysia backs regional push for stronger military cooperation, joint drills at Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 30— Asean defence ministers today agreed to step up joint military training and exercises, particularly in humanitarian assistance and disaster relief operations. According to Singapore’s Defence Ministry (Mindef), the ministers exchanged views on expanding cooperation through initiatives such as Indonesia’s Exercise Trident Resolve, scheduled for September 2026 and involving Asean Defence Ministers’ Meeting Plus (ADMM-Plus) countries, as well as enhanced information-sharing through platforms such as the Republic of Singapore Navy’s Information Fusion Centre. The meeting was co-hosted by Singapore Defence Minister Chan Chun Sing and Philippines Secretary of National Defence Gilberto Teodoro Jr, and attended by defence leaders and representatives from Asean member states.

items.title: US-Iran talks not a definitive fix for global energy market uncertainty, says Akmal
items.summary: He said developments in the Middle East continue to warrant close attention as the geopolitical situation remains fluid and could still affect global oil supplies. Akmal noted that Brent crude prices had fallen and remained below US$90 a barrel last week, while spot oil prices stayed under US$100 a barrel. Any disruption along the route, he said, could affect global energy supply chains and destabilise oil prices.

items.title: 'True heroine': Senator praises USM graduate for viral act of cross-cultural kindness
items.summary: He said an underprivileged man had been approaching patrons and seeking assistance. Lingeshwaran said a young woman, identified as Siti Zulaiqah, a graduate of Universiti Sains Malaysia, stepped forward to help. Describing Siti as an example of kindness in action, Lingeshwaran said meaningful acts did not require wealth, power or status.

items.title: PM Anwar’s Aidiladha contribution benefits 2,000 Orang Asli residents in Ulu Kinta
items.summary: Tambun parliamentary special officer Mohamad Hairul Amir Sabri said the programme was organised by the Tambun Parliamentary Community Service Centre in collaboration with the Department of Orang Asli Development (JAKOA), with the participation of the Orang Asli community. The programme involved Orang Asli community from Kampung Makmur, Kampung Tonggang, Kampung Chadak, Kampung Suluh, Kampung Suak Petai, Kampung Choh and Kampung Baduk. Meanwhile, Kampung Makmur Tok Batin Jailani Rasid said the programme benefited the Orang Asli community while strengthening ties among residents.

items.title: Public Service Dept warns action may be taken against those spreading false info about govt agencies
items.summary: KAMPAR, May 30 — Individuals found spreading videos or false information that tarnish the image of government ministries or agencies may face action. Public Service Department (PSD) deputy director-general (Operations) Datuk Mohd Shaiful Ibrahim said such action was necessary to protect the credibility of government departments, as false information could undermine public confidence in the civil service. On the programme, Mohd Shaiful said the PSD contributed five cows for the korban (sacrificial ritual), which involved about 100 participants comprising PSD staff, personnel from the Kampar District and Land Office, and locals.

items.title: Kedah Domestic Trade Ministry probes three fleet card misuse cases
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 30 — The Kedah Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) is investigating three cases of fleet card misuse and reviewing companies granted the facility to prevent abuse of subsidised fuel. Kedah KPDN chief enforcement officer Zalina Aziz said the ministry is also reassessing companies provided with fleet card facilities to ensure they are not misused or in breach of stipulated conditions. Zalina said Kedah KPDN currently has about 190 enforcement personnel across five branches statewide, but continued to face constraints in monitoring all 362 petrol stations in the state.

items.title: Man arrested after viral road rage incident in Bayan Lepas
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police have arrested a 37-year-old man following a road rage incident where he allegedly criminally intimidated another driver and made obscene gestures along Jalan Tun Dr Awang on Thursday. Southwest district police chief Assistant Commissioner Anuwal Ab Wahab said the incident occurred at 11.27am when the suspect, driving a Perodua Myvi, abruptly cut in front of the complainant's vehicle before stopping. Anuwal said police arrested the suspect following investigations into the incident.

items.title: Police confirm Perlis MB’s shotgun legally licensed, seize weapon after viral sacrificial cow shooting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah holds a valid firearms licence for the shotgun used to shoot a sacrificial cow that had reportedly turned aggressive, police said, after a video of the incident sparked debate online. In a statement today, Kangar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said checks by the district police licensing division confirmed that the firearm was legally registered to Abu Bakar. Yusharifuddin said officers had recorded Abu Bakar’s statement.

items.title: Devotees flock to Penang temples ahead of Wesak as grand floral float nears completion
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Thousands of devotees have flocked to Buddhist temples across Penang ahead of Wesak Day tomorrow, as final touches are being made to the state's main procession float. Devotees lit joss sticks and offered prayers inside the temple, while outside, teams of volunteers carefully arranged thousands of flowers that will adorn the float when it takes to the streets of George Town. Floral arrangement adviser Chin Lee Siew, 80, said preparations began on Thursday and intensified after flowers started arriving two days ago.

items.title: US warns capable of resuming war with Iran as deal remains elusive
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States warned on Saturday it was "more than capable" of resuming war with Iran after President Donald Trump said any peace deal must adhere to his red lines, including Teheran never being able to develop nuclear weapons. US sources had told AFP the deal was waiting on Trump's sign-off, but the president had made no decision after a two-hour meeting in the White House Situation Room on Friday in Washington. "Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon," the official added.

items.title: Anwar hails Tabung Haji after fifth straight Labbaitom Award win for excellence in pilgrim services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) on its achievement in receiving the Labbaitom Award from the Saudi Arabian Ministry of Haj and Umrah for the fifth consecutive time. In a Facebook post today, he said the achievement recognises Malaysia’s excellence in the management and welfare of Haj pilgrims. The Labbaitom Award is an annual recognition presented by the Saudi Arabian Ministry of Haj and Umrah to Haj operators worldwide who demonstrate outstanding performance in Haj management.

items.title: Derailed train cars cleared as Rapid KL works to restore normal services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Four train cars that derailed recently near the Chan Sow Lin station have been successfully rerailed and returned to the depot for technical inspection. Rapid KL, in a Facebook post today, said recovery works were carried out yesterday and early this morning, involving the coupling of the train cars with a specialised track vehicle to enable their safe removal. On Thursday, a train on the LRT Ampang/Sri Petaling Line derailed near the station following a fault in a track switch, disrupting service schedules.

items.title: Police verify Perlis MB's firearm licence, seize shotgun
items.summary: KANGAR: Police have confirmed that Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah holds a valid licence for the shotgun used to shoot a rampaging cow on Thursday. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said checks by the district police headquarters' licensing division confirmed it. "A police team from the Kangar district headquarters recorded a statement from the menteri besar.

items.title: Sustained, intelligence-led crackdown needed to dismantle underground vape market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Enforcement authorities must adopt sustained, intelligence-led operations to dismantle the underground market for drug-laced vape products and halt vaping among schoolchildren. Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye said sporadic enforcement alone is insufficient to tackle the black market trade, which spans covert physical networks and online sales. "It requires sustained enforcement, stronger intelligence gathering, stricter penalties, tighter control of online sales, and close cooperation among various enforcement agencies," he said.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Sabah interim special grant increased to RM1.5 billion
items.summary: PENAMPANG: The interim special grant for Sabah has been increased to RM1.5 billion this year from RM600 million previously, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the increase is a temporary measure pending a final mechanism for Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63). The prime minister said he has tasked Second Finance Minister Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan and Treasury secretary-general Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican to refine negotiations with the Sabah state government.

items.title: Missing man sparks SAR operation, found asleep by washing machine next door
items.summary: PADAWAN: A man who triggered a search and rescue operation after disappearing from a wedding reception in Kampung Sira here early today was found asleep next to a neighbour's washing machine. A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesman said a distress call was received at 4.39am, prompting the deployment of a team from the Siburan fire station. Following a coordinated search effort, villagers discovered the man unhurt at 6am sleeping beside the household appliance next door.

items.title: Civil society groups seek stronger checks in AG-PP separation bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Civil society groups have called on the government to ensure that proposed constitutional amendments separating the roles of the Attorney-General (AG) and Public Prosecutor (PP) result in an independent, accountable and credible prosecution service. They said that while the bill was referred to the PSSC in March for further deliberation, neither the committee's report nor its proposed amendments had been made public. They called on the PSSC to immediately provide its report and proposed amendments to all MPs.

items.title: Domestic Trade Ministry launches Ops Kesan 6.0 to curb profiteering amid rising oil prices
items.summary: KPDN enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the operation was aimed at ensuring traders do not take advantage of rising costs of raw materials, operations and business to increase prices unreasonably. He said Ops Kesan 6.0 would also be conducted in anticipation of the gradual impact of the global supply crisis on supply chains, delivery costs and the prices of essential goods in the country. Azman said the enforcement action was carried out in accordance with the Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act (AKHAP) 2011 (Act 723), as well as the Price Control and Anti-Profiteering (Mechanism to Determine Unreasonably High Profit) Regulations 2018.

items.title: Government focused on protecting jobs as global supply crisis persists, says economy minister
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 30 — The government is focusing on keeping economic activity strong to safeguard jobs for the people amid a global supply crisis, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said the current priority is to ensure uninterrupted supply, particularly of the country’s oil supply, to support economic activity. On geopolitical developments in West Asia, Akmal Nasrullah said uncertainty remains despite indications of a decline in global oil prices.

items.title: Anwar congratulates Tabung Haji, Amal on Saudi recognition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) for receiving prestigious recognition from the Saudi Arabian Ministry of Hajj and Umrah. This, Anwar said, reflects Malaysia's excellence in the management and welfare of pilgrims. "This success reflects the commitment, professionalism and dedication of all TH staff and hajj officials who always strive to provide the best service in managing Malaysian pilgrims," he said in a social media post today.

items.title: Four derailed LRT train carriages returned to depot for inspection, says Rapid KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Four train carriages that derailed near Chan Sow Lin Station recently have been successfully placed back on the tracks and transported to the depot for inspection and further action by the technical team. In a Facebook post today, Rapid KL said recovery works carried out yesterday and in the early hours of this morning involved reconnecting the train carriages to a specialised track vehicle to enable their safe and controlled removal from the site. On Thursday, a train on the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line derailed near Chan Sow Lin Station due to a fault in a track switch, disrupting service schedules.

items.title: Rescuers continue Gunung Batu Putih search for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, no plans to call off mission
items.summary: Its director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said about 50 personnel from various agencies remain involved in the operation at the location. He said another eight personnel were deployed to the mountain summit this morning to facilitate the rotation of rescue teams. “We have continued the operation since May 24.

items.title: Family of five escapes unhurt as suspected 'tekong darat' crashes car into house
items.summary: TUMPAT: A family of five, including a 79-year-old grandmother and her two grandchildren, escaped unhurt this morning when a car driven by a suspected human trafficking agent crashed into their home. The incident occurred at Kampung Tok Malik near Wakaf Bharu at 7am. House owner Aminah Seman said she was reciting the Quran in her bedroom when she heard a loud noise outside the house.

items.title: Kazakhstan offers to take Iran's uranium stockpile, IAEA chief tells FT
items.summary: LONDON: Kazakhstan has offered to take Iran's uranium stockpile if the United States and Iran reach an accord on Teheran's contested nuclear programme, UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi told the Financial Times on Friday. US President Donald Trump has insisted that Iran must accept that it will not have a nuclear weapon and that the uranium is destroyed. Iran has insisted on its right to maintain a nuclear programme.

items.title: Kaamatan greetings pour in as ministers celebrate Sabah’s festival of unity, gratitude and togetherness
items.summary: Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said Kaamatan is a valuable cultural heritage of the people of Sabah and has long served as a source of strength for the community. ‘Kotobian Tadau Tagazo do Kaamatan’ (Happy Kaamatan Festival),” he said. Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said Kaamatan is not only a continuation of cultural heritage, but also an important platform to strengthen unity, harmony and environmental awareness.

items.title: Anwar announces RM1.5b interim grant for Sabah while 40pc entitlement talks continue
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced that Sabah’s interim special grant will be raised from RM600 million to RM1.5 billion next year. Anwar said the increase is a temporary measure while discussions continue on the state’s constitutional claim to 40 per cent of federal revenue collected in Sabah, according to The Star . At the event today, Anwar was accompanied by Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Huguan Siou, state ministers and local representatives.

items.title: Minister urges SMEs to tap RM5b relief fund as 20 banks join financing scheme
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, May 30 — Small and medium enterprise (SME) entrepreneurs in the country have been urged to seize the opportunity to apply for financing under the SME Stabilisation Relief Facility (SME SRF) provided by the government to help their businesses navigate current economic challenges. “As we are aware, Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) recently announced the RM5 billion SME SRF special financing facility, which is part of the government’s efforts to ease the burden on entrepreneurs and help them address the impact of global economic challenges, including the global energy supply crisis,” he said. Among the banks involved are Bank Islam Malaysia Berhad, Bank Pertanian Malaysia Berhad (Agrobank), Small Medium Enterprise Development Bank Malaysia Berhad (SME Bank), Standard Chartered Bank Malaysia Berhad / Standard Chartered Saadiq Berhad and United Overseas Bank (Malaysia) Berhad.

items.title: Malaysia’s dry season is coming — and Bomba is preparing early
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, May 30 — The Fire and Rescue Department of Malaysia (JBPM) is stepping up preparations for heatwaves and forest fires amid expectations of drier weather linked to the El Nino phenomenon. Its director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said the department held a special meeting with all state fire directors two weeks ago to ensure this year’s preparations are more comprehensive than before. Nor Hisham said a key focus in tackling forest fires is strengthening the implementation of National Disaster Management Agency (NADMA) directive on disaster management.

items.title: He stepped out just in time: Trailer driver cheats death in Gua Musang ravine plunge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — A trailer driver cheated death after exiting his vehicle moments before it suddenly rolled forward and plunged into a ravine along the Gua Musang-Lojing road in Kelantan. The dramatic incident unfolded at Km90 of the route at about 7.30am yesterday when the 30-year-old driver stopped his trailer after suspecting a brake failure. “The driver realised the trailer was experiencing brake problems and stopped the vehicle on the left shoulder of the road for an inspection.
